title: Goan Chicken Vindaloo

subs.name: pork shoulder (750 g, cut into 4 cm cubes) — the original vindaloo protein
subs.notes: increase simmering to 45 minutes for pork to become tender

ingredients.name: dried red chillies (Byadgi + 2 hot Guntur)
visual_checks: use fewer chillies for a medium-hot version
ingredients.notes: use fewer chillies for a medium-hot version

subs.name: reduce to 4 Byadgi chillies and add 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour
subs.notes: this produces a milder, family-friendly vindaloo

subs.name: tamarind water (2 tbsp pulp dissolved in 3 tbsp water)
subs.notes: the dish will taste different but still authentically Goan

steps.text: Soak the dried red chillies in 3 tbsp warm vinegar for 15 minutes until soft. Transfer to a blender with garlic, ginger, cumin, cloves, cinnamon, black pepper and turmeric. Grind to a fine, smooth paste — the colour should be deep brick-red.
Rub 2 tbsp of the vindaloo paste over the chicken pieces and marinate for at least 15 minutes (longer is better). Keep the remaining paste for the gravy.

Heat oil in a kadai over medium-high flame. Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring frequently, until they turn a deep golden-brown, about 15 minutes. Do not rush — the caramelised onions give body and sweetness.
Add the remaining vindaloo paste to the onions and stir-fry for 3 minutes until the oil separates and the paste smells roasted rather than raw.
Add the marinated chicken and any residual marinade. Increase heat to high and sear the pieces, turning, until every piece is coated in the red masala, about 5 minutes.

Add 200 ml water, season with salt and add jaggery if using. Cover and simmer on medium for 20 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the gravy is thick and intensely red. Stir once or twice to prevent sticking.
Taste for salt, heat and sourness — add a few more drops of vinegar if you want more tang. Vindaloo improves overnight; serve the next day if possible, with steamed rice or crusty Goan pav.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → pork shoulder (750 g, cut into 4 cm cubes) — the original vindaloo protein (increase simmering to 45 minutes for pork to become tender); vinegar → tamarind water (2 tbsp pulp dissolved in 3 tbsp water) (the dish will taste different but still authentically Goan); dried-red-chilli → reduce to 4 Byadgi chillies and add 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour (this produces a milder, family-friendly vindaloo)
